L1.area: Low-level archive creation and data compression utilities

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in a zip archive creation routine where excessively long filenames, comments, or extra fields are not validated against the 16-bit length limits of the zip format. This can result in integer truncation and a subsequent heap-based buffer overflow when processing crafted input.

L3.area: Minizip-derived zip archive creation utilities in the portability layer
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in a zip archive creation routine where excessively long filenames, comments, or extra fields provided when opening a new file entry within a zip archive are not validated against the 16-bit length limits of the zip format. The lengths are cast to narrower integer types without bounds checking, resulting in integer truncation and a subsequent heap-based buffer overflow.

ground_truth.reason: The zip archive creation function does not validate that filename, comment, and extra field lengths fit within 16-bit zip format limits, causing integer overflow and heap-based buffer overflow when excessively long values are provided.
